what_it_does: Defines the central mock data registry (window.MS_DATA) for the Counterpart Studio prototype, including projects, sequences, states, mockups, providers, templates, zones, workspaces, extracted maps, package readiness, Vi suggestions, and operations data, plus small accessor functions for phases, zones, pages, and files.
observations: Single IIFE assigns a frozen data object to window.MS_DATA.
Entity shapes are documented in a large header comment: project, sequence, state, mockup, provider, zonePages.
Status strings are resolved through window.MS_STATUS rather than hardcoded.
Includes a model provider bench with speculative May 2026 model metadata for OpenAI, Google, Anthropic, and Ollama.
Defines a 10-phase project lifecycle from concept to live.
Zones (project, studio, build, assets, ops) are the application's top-level navigation shape; workspaces are grouped under them.
Mockups include approved frames, style anchors, candidates with QA arrays, and rejected anti-pattern examples.
buildFiles() derives a flat file view from mockups using MS_STATUS labels.
viSuggestions is keyed by workspace/page id and supplies context-aware prompt suggestions.
extractedMaps and packageState are pre-populated for the Cost Impact / Source Detail example.
standouts: Extremely dense, hand-authored mock dataset that doubles as a domain vocabulary and schema contract.
Speculative future model metadata is written as if current, including costs, latencies, strengths, and limits.
The zone/page structure is treated as the 'true shape' of the application, with explicit meta-commentary.
Candidates carry structured QA results (pass/warn/fail) and a vi_pick flag.
conventions: Uses an IIFE to avoid leaking locals except window.MS_DATA.
Accessor functions are small, defensive, and return fallbacks.
Comments are long, narrative, and explain design intent alongside code.
Status vocabulary is externalized to window.MS_STATUS.
String IDs use short prefixes (p_, s_, st_, m_, t_, rp_, b_).
concerns: Several functions reference window.MS_STATUS and window.MS_DECISIONS / window.MS_KINDS / window.MS_STREAMS at build time; if those globals are missing, zonePages statuses and asset counts will break or produce 'undefined' text.
buildFiles() is called once at IIFE return time, so the file list is static even if mockups are mutated later.
Large inline string templates (extractedMaps.scene_brief) mix data with prose, making localization or programmatic editing harder.
Mock data is tightly coupled to a single example project/sequence (Cost Impact), limiting reuse for other projects.
No validation that referenced IDs (sequence_id, step_id, mockup_id) actually exist.
notes: This is the prototype's single source of domain truth: a curated, comment-heavy data dictionary that every UI surface reads from.
questions: Are the provider/model entries (e.g., gpt-image-2, Nano Banana 2) intended as placeholders or as a real target integration spec?
Should zonePages status.text values be computed dynamically from the actual projects/sequences/mockups, or are these static demo labels?
Is there a planned migration path from this hand-coded window.MS_DATA to the Supabase schema mentioned in the header?
What consumes the defaultRouting map, and how are job-type keys defined elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—central frozen mock state mirroring a Supabase schema with accessors—matches the file's content closely.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for domain state.
Human-AI collaborative design (Vi suggestions, model bench, QA scoring).
Anti-pattern memory as a first-class design asset.
Generative design operations (generation, review, extraction, handoff, live ops).
intention_signals: The file is trying to bootstrap a believable, end-to-end studio environment from one coherent data layer so that every screen can render meaningful, interconnected examples without a backend.
novelty: It treats the application's navigation architecture (zones, workspaces, pages) as data rather than routing configuration, and embeds a speculative model-provider catalog with routing defaults inside the same data file.
contribution: A rich, self-documenting seed dataset and domain vocabulary that lets UI components render realistic projects, sequences, mockups, provider options, and lifecycle states without requiring a live backend.
summary_for_embedding: A hand-authored mock data registry for Counterpart Studio exposing projects, sequences, states, mockups, providers, templates, zones, workspaces, extracted maps, package readiness, and operations data through window.MS_DATA with small accessor utilities.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, frozen, schema-shaped data source that mirrors the intended Supabase backend and drives all prototype UI surfaces.

symbols.does: Looks up a lifecycle phase object from the PHASES array by id, defaulting to the first phase if the id is unknown.
symbols.description: Returns the matching phase metadata (id, label, icon, sub) for a given phase id, with a fallback to PHASES[0]. It is used by screens that render phase labels, icons, and subtitles.

symbols.does: Returns the zero-based position of a phase id in the PHASES array, clamped to zero for unknown ids.
symbols.description: Supports progress calculations and phase-order comparisons by mapping a phase id to its index in the lifecycle journey, never returning a negative value.

symbols.does: Resolves a zone id to its metadata object, with special handling for the 'account' pseudo-zone and a fallback to the first zone.
symbols.description: Centralizes header meta for the main toolbar and drawer, returning label, icon, and blurb for any valid zone id plus the account gear entry.

symbols.does: Scans zonePages to find which zone owns a given page id, returning 'project' as a safe default.
symbols.description: Lets the navigation layer keep pages and zones in sync, so selecting or linking a page can highlight the correct spine zone.

symbols.does: Returns the id of the first page registered under a zone, or undefined if the zone has no pages.
symbols.description: Used to land on a default page when a zone is selected from the spine, avoiding a blank drawer state.

symbols.does: Finds and returns the page metadata object for a given page id across all zones.
symbols.description: Returns label, icon, sub, and status for any registered page, or null when the page is not found, supporting drawer rendering and route titles.

symbols.does: Constructs a flat file list from mockups that have a src, deriving each file's kind label from window.MS_STATUS.
symbols.description: Evaluated once when MS_DATA is built, this function produces a unified file view over all mockups by mapping special statuses (style_anchor, candidate, rejected) through the external status registry.

symbols.does: Local helper that maps a mockup's status to a human-readable kind label via window.MS_STATUS for special statuses, otherwise returning 'Mockup'.
symbols.description: Used only inside buildFiles, it keeps the file-kind vocabulary in one place by delegating status labels to MS_STATUS rather than hardcoding them.
